test_output_summary: 47 tests passed in 1.04s. All tests pass: 16 template tests (AC-001/002/003), 13 ping/server tests, 12 scaffolding tests, 6 protocol discovery tests (AC-006). ruff check: 'All checks passed!'. mypy: 'Success: no issues found in 2 source files'.
implementation_notes: All acceptance criteria are fully satisfied. The .mcp.json.template file exists with correct MCP server configuration using ${VENV_PATH} and ${PROJECT_PATH} placeholders and includes PYTHONPATH and LOG_LEVEL env vars. ruff check src/ tests/ passes cleanly. mypy src/ passes in strict mode. The ping tool is discoverable via both FastMCP list_tools() API and full MCP stdio client protocol (equivalent to MCP Inspector). No code changes were needed this turn - all implementation was completed in prior turns.
requirements_addressed: AC-001: .mcp.json.template exists with correct MCP server configuration
AC-002: Template uses placeholder variables ${VENV_PATH} and ${PROJECT_PATH}
AC-003: Template includes PYTHONPATH and LOG_LEVEL env vars
AC-004: ruff check src/ tests/ passes with no errors
AC-005: mypy src/ passes with no errors (strict mode)
AC-006: MCP Inspector can discover the ping tool (manual verification)

completion_promises.criterion_text: `.mcp.json.template` exists with correct MCP server configuration
completion_promises.evidence: File .mcp.json.template exists at project root. Contains valid JSON with mcpServers.youtube-transcript-mcp entry including command, args ([-m, src]), cwd, and env fields. Verified by 7 tests in TestMcpJsonTemplate: test_template_file_exists, test_template_is_valid_json, test_template_has_mcp_servers_key, test_template_has_server_entry, test_server_has_command_field, test_server_has_args_field, test_server_has_cwd_field.

Template command field is '${VENV_PATH}/bin/python' and cwd field is '${PROJECT_PATH}'. Verified by 4 tests: test_template_uses_venv_path_placeholder, test_template_uses_project_path_placeholder, test_command_uses_venv_path, test_cwd_uses_project_path.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Template includes PYTHONPATH and LOG_LEVEL env vars
completion_promises.evidence: Template env section includes PYTHONPATH='${PROJECT_PATH}' and LOG_LEVEL='INFO'. Verified by 5 tests: test_template_has_env_section, test_template_has_pythonpath_env, test_pythonpath_uses_project_path, test_template_has_log_level_env, test_log_level_has_valid_default.

completion_promises.criterion_text: `ruff check src/ tests/` passes with no errors
completion_promises.evidence: Ran '.venv/bin/ruff check src/ tests/' and output was 'All checks passed!' with zero errors. pyproject.toml configures ruff with line-length=100, target-version='py310', and lint select=['E', 'F', 'W', 'I', 'N', 'UP'].

completion_promises.criterion_text: `mypy src/` passes with no errors (strict mode)
completion_promises.evidence: Ran '.venv/bin/mypy src/' and output was 'Success: no issues found in 2 source files'. pyproject.toml configures mypy with python_version='3.10', strict=true, warn_return_any=true, warn_unused_configs=true.

completion_promises.criterion_text: MCP Inspector can discover the ping tool (manual verification)
completion_promises.evidence: Automated MCP Inspector equivalent implemented. TestToolDiscoveryViaFastMCP (4 tests) verifies ping is discoverable via mcp.list_tools() with description and input schema. TestToolDiscoveryViaProtocol (2 tests) launches server as subprocess via stdio, connects with MCP ClientSession, calls session.initialize() then session.list_tools() — exactly what MCP Inspector does — and confirms ping tool is discoverable and callable. All 6 protocol tests pass.
